title: Foldable tube with unitary hinge devices, systems, and methods
abstract: Methods, systems, and devices for foldable tube with unitary hinge are provided in accordance with various embodiments. Some embodiments include a device that may include one or more foldable tube sections configured to facilitate the deployment of an object with respect to an end portion of at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections; each of the one or more foldable tube sections may include one or more unitary hinge regions. The unitary hinge regions may include one or more longitudinal hinge regions configured to enable folding that is transverse to a primary axis of a respective foldable tube section. The unitary hinge regions may include a lateral hinge region configured to enable flattening that is parallel to a primary axis of a respective foldable tube section. Some embodiments may include a wire harness disposed within an interior portion of one or more foldable tube sections.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A device comprising: one or more foldable tube sections configured to facilitate the deployment of an object with respect to an end portion of at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections, wherein each of the one or more foldable tube sections includes one or more unitary hinge regions. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more foldable tube sections include a composite material with a plurality of layers, wherein the plurality of layers includes one or more carbon fiber layers. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein at least one of the unitary hinge regions includes a longitudinal hinge region configured to enable folding that is transverse to a primary axis of a respective foldable tube section. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the longitudinal hinge region includes at least a portion of the one or more carbon fiber layers. 5. The device of claim 3, further comprising one or more circumferential bands proximal to the longitudinal hinge region. 6. The device of claim 5, wherein the one or more circumferential bands are formed from one or more composite material layers. 7. The device of claim 3, wherein the longitudinal hinge region includes two or more apertures configured to allow the respective foldable tube section to fold and to flatten at least a portion of the longitudinal hinge region. 8. The device of claim 3, wherein the one or more foldable tube sections are fabricated to compress along at least a portion of the one of the more foldable tube sections different from the longitudinal hinge region. 9. The device of claim 8, wherein at least one of the unitary hinge regions includes a lateral hinge region configured to enable flattening that is parallel to a primary axis of a respective foldable tube section. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the lateral hinge region includes one or more layers devoid of carbon fibers. 11. The device of claim 3, wherein the longitudinal hinge region includes one or more relief slots oriented around a circumference of a respective foldable tube section. 12. The device of claim 11, wherein the one or more relief slots allow the longitudinal hinge region to self-buckle. 13. The device of claim 3, further comprising a wire harness disposed within an interior portion of at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections. 14. The device of claim 13, wherein the wire harness includes one or more reduced regions configured to coincide with one or more of the respective longitudinal hinge regions. 15. The device of claim 13, wherein the wire harness is configured to at least fold or flex laterally in a region of the one or more foldable tube sections other than the longitudinal hinge region. 16. The device of claim 13, wherein the wire harness includes one or more relief slots. 17. The device of claim 1, wherein a first foldable tube section from the one or more foldable tube sections is configured to stack with a second foldable tube section from the one or more foldable tube sections when at least one of the one or more unitary hinge regions is folded and the first foldable tube section and the second foldable tube section are compressed. 18. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more foldable tube sections form a yoke configuration, wherein the yoke configuration includes two foldable tube sections from the one or more foldable tube sections that form two arm portions and one foldable tube section that forms a base portion. 19. The device of claim 18, wherein each of the two arm portions includes one or more unitary hinge regions and the base portion includes one or more unitary hinge regions. 20. The device of claim 19, wherein the two arm portions and the base portion are configured to avoid overlapping when the one or more unitary hinge regions are folded. 21. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more foldable tube sections are fabricated to compress to provide a kickoff spring functionality for deployment of the one or more foldable tube sections. 22. The device of claim 1, further comprising one or more tube interfaces coupled with one or more end portions of at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections. 23. The device of claim 22, wherein the object includes one or more solar panels coupled with at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections utilizing at least one of the one or more tube interfaces. 24. The device of claim 1, further comprising one or more saddle components configured to constrain at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections during at least a folding or a compressing of the one or more foldable tube sections. 25. A method comprising: folding one or more foldable tube sections with respect to one or more longitudinal unitary hinge regions of the one or more foldable tube sections to stow the one or more foldable tube sections, wherein the one or more foldable tube sections are configured to facilitate the deployment of an object with respect to an end portion at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections. 26. The method of 25, further comprising: compressing the one or more foldable tube sections with respect to one or more longitudinal portions of the one or more foldable tube sections to further stow the one or more foldable tube sections. 27. The method of claim 26, further comprising: deploying the one or more foldable tube sections through decompressing the one or more compressed longitudinal portions of the one or more foldable tube sections and unfolding the one or more foldable tube sections with respect to the one or more longitudinal unitary hinge regions. 28. The method of claim 26, wherein at least folding or compressing the one or more foldable tube sections stacks the one or more longitudinal portions of the one or more foldable tube sections 29. The method of claim 25, wherein folding the one or more foldable tube sections avoids overlapping one or more longitudinal portions of the one or more foldable tube sections. 30. The method of claim 26, wherein compressing the one or more foldable tube sections provides a kickoff spring functionality for deploying the one or more foldable tube sections. 31. The method of claim 26, wherein compressing the one or more foldable tube sections includes flattening the one or more foldable tube sections with respect to one or more lateral unitary hinge regions. 32. The method of claim 27, wherein deploying the one or more foldable tube sections includes deploying an object coupled with an end portion of at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections. 33. The method of claim 27, further comprising utilizing one or more relief slots of a respective longitudinal unitary hinge region to allow for over rotation of the longitudinal unitary hinge region during deployment. 34. The method of claim 27, further comprising utilizing one or more circumferential bands proximal to at least one of the one or more longitudinal unitary hinge regions. 35. The method of claim 25, further comprising utilizing a wire harness disposed within an interior portion of at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections. 36. The method of claim 26, further comprising constraining one or more portions of the one or more tube sections during at least the folding or the compressing utilizing one or more saddle components. 37. The method of claim 25, wherein the one or more foldable tube sections include a composite material with a plurality of layers, wherein the plurality of layers includes one or more carbon fiber layers. 38. The method of claim 25, wherein the one or more longitudinal unitary hinge regions include two or more apertures configured to allow the respective foldable tube section to fold and to flatten at least a portion of the respective longitudinal unitary hinge region. 39. The method of claim 31, wherein the one or more lateral unitary hinge regions include one or more layers devoid of carbon fibers. 40. The method of claim 33, wherein the one or more relief slots allow the longitudinal hinge region to self-buckle. 41. The method of claim 35, wherein the wire harness includes one or more reduced regions configured to coincide with one or more of the longitudinal unitary hinge regions. 42. The method of claim 35, wherein the wire harness is configured to at least fold or flex laterally in a region of the one or more foldable tube sections other than the one or more longitudinal unitary hinge regions. 43. The method of claim 35, wherein the wire harness includes one or more relief slots. 44. The method of claim 32, wherein the object includes one or more solar panels coupled with at least one of the one or more foldable tube sections utilizing at least one of the one or more tube interfaces.
